channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How President Trump is expanding the powers of the presidency
item.description: President Trump is pushing the limits of presidential power, and the courts are pushing back. Coming up at 9 a.m. on Monday, MPR News host Angela Davis talks about what a president can and cannot do under the law.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Maja Beckstrom, MPR News

item.title: Top Trump agency recovers eye-popping sum after launching DOGE task force
item.description: Scott Turner Department of Housing and Urban Development secretary, announced a new Department of Government Efficiency Task Force Thursday to “eliminate waste, fraud and abuse" at HUD.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump plans to attend NASCAR's Daytona 500 for the second time as president
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump is planning to attend Sunday's Daytona 500 for the second time as president and help further burnish his image as a sports fan. He was at the race in 2020 while running for a second term.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell's mental acuity targeted by Trump after ex-Senate leader joins Dems against Cabinet nominees
item.description: The GOP's recent and longest-serving Senate party leader has stood in opposition to his conference multiple times, demonstrating the party’s significant transformation in the age of Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: George has his day, and so does Abe. But states honor US presidents in lots of ways
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Is Presidents Day the most confusing holiday in the U.S.? States seem to have as many names for it and ideas about whom to honor as there have been presidents. The federal government doesn't even recognize Monday as Presidents Day.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Presidents Day has evolved from reverence to retail
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Like the other Founding Fathers, George Washington was uneasy about the idea of publicly celebrating his life. He was the first leader of a new republic, not a king. And yet the United States will once again commemorate its first president on Monday, 293 years after he was born.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Area 51: Fact and fiction
item.description: The Groom Lake, Nevada, facility — about 100 miles northwest of Las Vegas — remains highly secretive.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: IRS to slash thousands of workers off the payroll: report
item.description: The Associated Press reported the IRS will cut thousands of employees as the Trump administration trims the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon's America: What Elon Musk did before DOGE
item.description: Elon Musk may be the face of DOGE, but he made a name for himself in businesses built around technology and space travel.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats nervously wait on Trump response to court rulings
item.description: House Democrats howling over the flood of unilateral actions streaming from the White House are voicing confidence that the courts will provide a check on potential abuses of power. They’re less certain, however, about how President Trump will respond.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season
item.description: The IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season, according to two sources familiar with the agency’s plans, and cuts could come as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hungary's transformation into an 'electoral autocracy' has parallels to Trump's second term
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hours before President Donald Trump was sworn in to begin his second term, promising a “golden age” for America, the leader of a Central European country was describing the years ahead in strikingly similar terms.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Planned Parenthood appears to scrub Instagram as fears of DOGE cuts loom
item.description: Planned Parenthood seemed to scrub all content from its Instagram shortly after HHS Secretary RFK Jr.'s swearing in without an explanation. Some believe the organization fears DOGE cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Legislative immunity is a privilege in most states. A speeding ticket could change that in Arizona
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — When a police officer gave state Sen. Mark Finchem a speeding ticket, the Arizona Republican didn't need to worry about the consequences.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's what happened during President Donald Trump's 4th week in office
item.description: President Donald Trump and his administration are working to negotiate an end to the war between Russia and Ukraine, and everything else that happened his fourth week in his second administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PIX on Politics Panel breaks down Adams-DOJ controversy
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It’s been a politically charged week for Mayor Eric Adams after the Department of Justice ordered prosecutors to drop a federal corruption case against him.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Justice Department asks court to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Justice Department asked a court Friday to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams, with a top official from Washington intervening after federal prosecutors in Manhattan rebuffed his demands to drop the case and some quit in protest.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, LARRY NEUMEISTER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's framed mug shot hangs outside Oval Office
item.description: A framed image of President Donald Trump's mug shot from when he was indicted in Georgia in 2023 is hanging outside the Oval Office in the latest bit of redecorating since he moved back in.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump creates council for 'energy dominance,' boosts natural gas exports and offshore drilling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday signed an executive order formally creating a National Energy Dominance Council and directed it to move quickly to drive up already record-setting domestic oil and gas production.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance meets with leader of far-right German party
item.description: Vice President Vance met Friday with a leader of the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party, making him the highest-ranking U.S. official to do so.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Thursday threatened to sue Google over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Donald Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A federal worker tried to take Trump's 'Fork' resignation offer. Here's what happened
item.description: Liz Goggin, a social worker with the Veterans Health Administration, took the offer to resign in exchange for pay and benefits through September. Then she learned her position was exempt.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Order to drop New York Mayor Adams' case roils Justice Department as high-ranking officials resign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Manhattan’s top federal prosecutor, Danielle Sassoon, and five high-ranking Justice Department officials resigned Thursday after she refused an order to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams — a stunning escalation in a dayslong standoff over the Trump admini
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Denver Public Schools sues DHS over policy allowing ICE in schools
item.description: Denver Public Schools (DPS) sued the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) on Wednesday over its new Trump administration policy allowing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to conduct raids in schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Minneapolis City Council postpones proposal to move violence prevention funds to county
item.description: After a lengthy and sometimes heated discussion, the Minneapolis City Council voted Thursday to indefinitely postpone a proposal to have Hennepin County temporarily oversee two of the city’s violence intervention programs.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota lawmaker wants to ban new billboard signs
item.description: Sen. John Marty, DFL-Roseville, said the signs detract from the state's natural scenic beauty and pose a safety concern for motorists. Billboard companies say the bill would hurt local businesses and curtail First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Legislature OKs bill targeting high egg prices, allowing suspension of cage-free requirement
item.description: Nevada lawmakers on Wednesday gave final approval to a bill that could bring down the price of eggs, clearing the way for Gov. Joe Lombardo to give it his signature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gov. Hochul reacts to pending dismissal of corruption case against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The pending dismissal of the corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams has partners in running the city and political opponents raising concerns about whether Adams can run the city. Gov. Kathy Hochul even said she does not know if the mayor is “compromised.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Battleground Florida: Officials discuss tariffs, LGBTQ policies
item.description: University of Tampa Economics Professor Michael Coon explained the purpose of tariffs and the impact they will likely have on businesses and families in Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Black educators warn of what is lost if Education Department is shuttered
item.description: Black educators are sounding the alarm as President Donald Trump ramps up talk of eliminating the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5
